citations.description: Ticket comment by RebarBotNava: 'AC2 (durable config_manual pending-marker in autodeploy.sh) remains — that is the code portion of this ticket.'

findings.criterion: config_manual signals persist (re-emitted or alarmed) until acknowledged.
findings.detail: autodeploy.sh lines 173-177 emit `err config_manual` exactly once when a CONFIG_PATHS change is detected in the current DEPLOYED..TARGET range, then the deployed-sha is unconditionally advanced (line 263). There is no durable state file tracking a pending manual apply, no re-emission on subsequent runs, and no alarm/acknowledgement mechanism. The fire-and-forget defect described in the bug is not fixed. The ticket's own comment explicitly confirms 'AC2 (durable config_manual pending-marker in autodeploy.sh) remains — that is the code portion of this ticket.'
findings.remediation: Add a persistent state file (e.g. $STATE_DIR/pending-config-manual) that is written when a config_manual event is detected and cleared only when an operator explicitly acknowledges it. Re-emit the config_manual error signal on every autodeploy run while the state file is present, before advancing deployed-sha.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
